DEBUGGING.description: Diagnosing failures, tracebacks, regressions, incorrect outputs, or broken behavior.

ERROR_RECOVERY.description: Recovering from failed commands, invalid assumptions, tool errors, retries, or partial progress.

TERMINAL_WORKFLOW.description: Using shell commands, command output, scripts, test runners, package managers, or CLIs.

TOOL_USE.description: Calling tools/functions and conditioning on observations or tool responses.

CODEBASE_NAVIGATION.description: Searching, reading, locating, and understanding files or symbols in a repository.

MULTI_FILE_EDITING.description: Coordinated changes across multiple files, modules, tests, or interfaces.

TEST_WRITING.description: Creating, updating, or reasoning about tests, fixtures, assertions, and coverage.

PLANNING.description: Decomposing goals, sequencing work, producing implementation plans, or choosing strategy.

RETRIEVAL_SEARCH.description: Searching or retrieving external/internal information, documentation, references, or facts.

CODE_EDITING.description: Producing patches, diffs, implementations, edits, or code modifications.

COMPOSITION.description: Drafting prose, essays, narratives, explanations, dialogue, arguments, or other written artifacts.

REVISION.description: Revising, editing, improving clarity, tone, structure, style, or rhetoric in prose.

STRUCTURED_REASONING.description: Explicit stepwise reasoning, analysis, derivations, rationale, or structured thinking.

notes: Do not split by programming language or framework.
Create matched general-agent and specialist subsets for every comparison.
Use these normalized labels across all rollout sources, even when source datasets expose different category names.
